personas.persona: Non-technical founder who wants a working web app fast.
prompts: Build me a simple to-do list web app: add tasks, mark them complete, delete them, and keep them saved in the browser between reloads. Then tell me how to open it.
Build a clean single-page marketing site for a product called 'Focus' with a hero headline, three feature cards, and an email signup form. Show me how to run it locally.
Build a small weather dashboard web page where I type a city and it shows a mocked 5-day forecast as cards. Plain HTML/CSS/JS, no build step.

personas.persona: Writer who wants a simple notes tool.
prompts: Build a single-page markdown notes app: a textarea on the left, a live-rendered HTML preview on the right, and notes saved in localStorage.
Build a tiny URL shortener web app using only Python's standard library for the backend plus a simple HTML form. Show me how to run and test it.

Help me create a task management solution for my 4-person company. I want it to be AI-native, meaning the content should be editable via natural language. however only one person should be able to edit the codebase (ie the structure of the dashboard, the ui, etc) to eliminate the chance of a non-technical team member accidentally breaking the dashboard.

I want to surface 25 unique profiles from GitHub of engineers that are passionate open source contributors. Ideally, they're located in the Bay Area and have projects that are over 200 stars. They should also have actively contributed to a project within the last month

We have designed a hosting service for small apps meant for personal use — the opposite of scale. The idea is that people can deploy their own apps (usually open source) instead of relying on third party Saas. I would like you to develop a tool for me where I can provide potential names and it will do a trademark and competitive scan (including things like domain availability). I also want to be able to compare names after the tool has done these assessments.
